truth.papers.A70.title: Nano-mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels
truth.papers.A70.paper_scope: Investigate nanomechanical properties of two duplex low-density steels through nano-indentation and DFT, elucidating the effect of annealing temperature and Ni addition on the hardness of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics.

prediction.papers.A70.title: Nano-mechanical properties of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels
prediction.papers.A70.paper_scope: Nano-mechanical properties of duplex Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steels with and without 5 wt.% Ni as a function of annealing temperature (500–1050°C), focusing on the contributions of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallic phases through nano-indentation and ab-initio DFT calculations.

truth.structures.sample_5ni_st.overall_structu: Dual-phase (γ + α) after solution treatment and quenching.
truth.structures.sample_5ni_500.overall_struct: Austenitic matrix with cuboid κ-carbides (<100 nm), B2 stringer bands (replacing α), and coarse grain boundary B2 particles (2–7 μm).
truth.structures.sample_5ni_700.overall_struct: Similar to 500 °C: γ matrix with cuboid κ, B2 stringer bands, coarse GB B2 particles.
truth.structures.sample_5ni_900.overall_struct: Dual-phase γ + α. γ contains coarse B2 particles (2–7 μm); α contains disc-like B2 (≈200 nm).
truth.structures.sample_5ni_1050.overall_struc: Similar to 900 °C: γ + α; coarse B2 (2–4 μm) in γ, disc-like B2 (≈200 nm) in α.
truth.structures.sample_0c_st.overall_structur: Dual-phase γ + α after solution treatment and quench.
truth.structures.sample_0c_500.overall_structu: γ matrix with coarse B2 particles (2–4 μm); α matrix with needle-like κ (width 200±33 nm).
truth.structures.sample_0c_700.overall_structu: γ + α; coarse B2 in γ, needle-like κ (width 500±33 nm) in α.
truth.structures.sample_0c_900.overall_structu: γ + α; coarse B2 (2–4 μm) in γ, disc-like B2 (≈200 nm) in α.
truth.structures.sample_0c_1050.overall_struct: γ + α; coarse B2 (2–4 μm) in γ, disc-like B2 (≈200 nm) in α.

prediction.structures.sample_5ni_500.overall_struct: Duplex-like matrix replaced by γ-phase containing cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, with prior α-phase regions transformed to B2 stringer bands at 500°C.
prediction.structures.sample_5ni_700.overall_struct: γ-phase matrix with cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, and B2 stringer bands replacing the prior α-phase at 700°C.
prediction.structures.sample_5ni_900.overall_struct: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 900°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
prediction.structures.sample_5ni_1050.overall_struc: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 1050°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
prediction.structures.sample_0c_500.overall_structu: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 500°C; γ contains cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, α contains needle-like κ-carbides.
prediction.structures.sample_0c_700.overall_structu: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 700°C; γ contains cuboid κ-carbides and coarse B2 particles, α contains needle-like κ-carbides.
prediction.structures.sample_0c_900.overall_structu: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 900°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.
prediction.structures.sample_0c_1050.overall_struct: Duplex microstructure of γ and α at 1050°C; γ contains coarse B2 particles and α contains nano-sized disc-like B2 precipitates.

truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.purpose: Microstructure observation after polishing and etching in 5% nital
truth.characterization_methods.idx_0.key_findi: Revealed phases as described in Table 1 and Figure 2.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.purpose: Selected-area diffraction patterns and bright-field imaging for phase identification
truth.characterization_methods.idx_1.key_findi: Imaged disc-like B2, cuboid κ, and determined phase relationships.
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.purpose: Measure hardness of individual phases and composite regions
truth.characterization_methods.idx_2.key_findi: Hardness values recorded in Table 2 (not provided in text); Ni-containing samples higher hardness due to NiAl B2; B2 stringer bands had lower hardness than nano-discs; needle κ hardness function of Mn content.

truth.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: DFT (Density Functional Theory) using Quantum-ESPRESSO package
truth.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: spin-polarized GGA-PBE; ultrasoft pseudopotentials; 18x18x18 k-points for BZ; 30x30x30 for DOS; bulk modulus fitted with Murnaghan equation of state.
determine Young's modulus of κ-carbides as function of Mn content
Calculated elastic properties of κ-carbides and B2 (FeAl-L1₂); studied role of Mn in κ structure.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.simulation_m: Density Functional Theory (DFT) with generalized gradient approximation (GGA-PBE) in spin-polarized formalism
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.potential_mo: Ultrasoft pseudo-potentials based on modified RRKJ scheme
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.validation_a: Calculated elastic properties of κ-carbides and B2 intermetallics compared with nano-indentation trends; used to rationalize hardness differences between NiAl-type and FeAl-type B2 and Mn-content dependence of κ-phase.
prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Fitting simulated unit cell volumes to calculate bulk modulus
Various unit cell volumes were simulated and fitted to obtain bulk modulus.
Used to derive dynamical matrices from electronic subsystem linear response.
30×30×30 mesh used for electronic density of states.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Table 2 containing quantitative nano-hardness values of all phases and composites is referred to but not included in the provided text; all hardness comparisons are qualitative.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The paper reports that at lower annealing temperatures (500–700 °C) ductility was low (~10% elongation), and increasing annealing temperature improved strength-ductility balance.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Ni-containing samples had higher hardness due to NiAl-type B2 with stronger Ni-Al bonds compared to Fe-Al bonds in Ni-free samples.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: At ≥900 °C, α phase contained nano-sized disc B2; Ni-containing discs exhibited higher hardness.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: At ≤700 °C, Ni-free sample formed micrometre-sized needle κ in α, lowering hardness; Ni-containing sample replaced α with B2 stringer bands, which had lower hardness than Ni-Al nano-discs.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: Hardness of needle-like κ in α was a function of Mn content.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: Cuboid κ in γ could not be measured individually; composite γ+κ hardness reported.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: DFT calculations provided Young's modulus of κ-carbides; yet no explicit values were extracted from text for the mechanical properties section.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: Figure 4 load-depth curves and hardness values not available as numerical data; only trends described.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_9.value: Table 1 microstructural description used to structure the phases; carbon content discrepancy (0.5C in table vs 0.8C in text) noted.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Table 1 lists the nominal carbon content as 0.5 wt.% for both alloys, whereas the main text and Materials section consistently specify 0.8 wt.%.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: Figure 4 and Table 2 contain quantitative nano-indentation hardness values and load-depth curves for various individual and composite phases, but the specific numerical data were not included in the provided text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Nano-indentation measured compound hardness of the following combined phases because individual nano-sized phase properties could not be isolated: γ + cuboid κ, γ + cuboid κ + microsized B2, γ + microsized B2, α + needle-shape κ, and α + nano-disk B2.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: DFT calculations determined the elastic constants and Young's modulus of nano-sized cuboid κ-phase as a function of Mn content, but specific numerical values for elastic constants or moduli were not provided in the text.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_4.value: Qualitative hardness trends indicate that Ni-containing samples exhibit higher hardness due to NiAl-type B2 compared with FeAl-type B2 in Ni-free samples, because Ni-Al bonds are stronger than Fe-Al bonds.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_5.value: At annealing temperatures of 900°C and above, nano-sized B2 discs in the ferrite phase yield higher hardness in Ni-containing samples than in Ni-free samples.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_6.value: At 700°C and below, Ni-free samples show lowered hardness because micrometre-sized needles of κ-carbide replace the nano-sized B2 discs in ferrite.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_7.value: In Ni-containing samples at 700°C and below, the prior ferrite phase is replaced by B2 stringer bands that have lower hardness than the Ni-Al nano-discs formed at higher temperatures, attributed to lower Ni content in the stringer bands.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_8.value: The hardness of needle-like κ-carbides formed in the α phase was reported to be a function of Mn content, but quantitative hardness values were not provided in the text.
